mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 369.9B USD in Dec 2006 reflect sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 3+ months, monetary tightening constrains equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 5.24% in Dec 2006 reflects tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite elevated FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires joint observation of both labor series to activate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll employment at 136.2M in Dec 2006 with unemployment at 4.5%
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to outperform single-series PAYEMS or UNRATE signals (Rank IC difference < 0.01), or negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 112.32 in Nov 2006
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to produce negative 5d returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,500K units annualized, signaling deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 1,588K in Nov 2006, near threshold
failure_condition: HOUST below 1,500K fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals monetary policy constraint and real rate tightening, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both inflation acceleration and high policy rates to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 201.7 in Nov 2006 with FEDFUNDS at 5.24%
failure_condition: Interaction signal underperforms single-series signals (Rank IC difference < 0.01), or negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals tight labor markets and wage inflation pressure, which the Federal Reserve must combat with sustained high rates, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 4.5% in Dec 2006, at threshold
regime_hint: tight labor market with policy constraint
failure_condition: UNRATE below 4.5% fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration below 2.5% fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
